title: Word Search II
problem_statement: Given a grid of letters and a list of words, return all words that can be formed by tracing adjacent letters.

examples.explanation: Only "eat" and "oath" can be formed from adjacent letters on the board.

intuition: Combine a trie for word lookup with DFS on the board to prune invalid paths early.

approaches.name: Trie plus DFS backtracking
approaches.description: Build a trie from the words, then perform DFS from each cell while traversing trie nodes and collecting complete words.
approaches.time: O(m * 4^L) worst-case, but pruning improves it significantly
approaches.space: O(total letters in words + board size)
approaches.why_not: Searching each word individually on the board is too slow for many words.

interview_qa.q: Why combine a trie with DFS?
interview_qa.a: The trie prunes word search branches early and avoids repeated work for shared prefixes.
After adding a word to the result, its trie node word field is set to null.
